name: Chocolate Slice
recipeInstructions:
Line a 14x10 inch cake pan with waxed paper and grease it. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Cake: Melt chocolate in a double boiler over low heat. In a large bowl, cream butter and half the sugar until light and fluffy. Add salt, cinnamon, and lemon peel. Beat in egg yolks one at a time, with melted chocolate. Beat egg whites until stiff; fold in remaining sugar. Fold egg white mixture into creamed mixture with flour. Spread batter evenly in prepared cake pan. Bake 20 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Sprinkle a sheet of waxed paper with sugar and turn out cake onto it. Peel off lining paper. Cool for 2 hours, then cut cooled cake lengthwise into two 14x5 strips.
Frosting: Melt butter, cool slightly. Finely grate chocolate. Mix chocolate, eggs, powdered sugar, butter, and rum in a double boiler. Stir constantly over low heat until chocolate has melted and all ingredients are thoroughly combined. Cool. Place the two 14x5 strips on top of one another, with about half of the frosting used as filling. Spread remaining frosting thickly over top. Cut into slices when frosting is firm.
